title: Luxury Goods | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
Whether you support a high-end fashion house, an up-and-coming independent jewelry designer, an upmarket accessories brand or an exclusive hospitality group, your work within the luxury market is constant, complex and demanding. As the representative of a luxury brand, there are endless details that require your attention—including decisions involving international business immigration.
We understand the importance of those details and that getting them wrong can mean missing out on a last-minute opportunity to showcase your brand, or that one of your executive consultants misses a crucial meeting overseas. You don’t want your brand’s profile—or your personal reputation—undermined by poor immigration planning.
Instead, let us handle the details for you. At Fragomen, we represent luxury brands, holding companies specializing in luxury goods and individual designers and executives that define the industry. We’ve served their immigration needs in countless scenarios—from a long-term placement of a top executive into a new market, to last-minute scrambles moving a design team to an event on another continent, and everything in between.
Even if you’ve never engaged with immigration counsel before, reach out to us. We’ll handle the details for you, and we’ll get them right—so that you and your people can continue with the work that matters.
Clients Value Us For:
You can expect the same high-level care and attention to detail from Fragomen that your customers expect from you. Our reputation as the world’s leading immigration firm is based on providing personalized attention that perfectly fits our clients’ needs.
From European fashion capitals to emerging markets in the Middle East and Asia, you’re establishing your brand in the most important, fashion-forward regions in the world. With support in 170 countries worldwide, we have the reach—and the experience—to move your people wherever they need to be, whenever they need to be there.
Advancing a luxury brand in today’s marketplace demands speed and flexibility. Whether we’re designing a last-minute immigration process for one of your directors or executives, or creating a program to cover your entire product development department, we can adapt our expertise to any circumstance. You won’t have to think about anything except the results we achieve—and the results will be exceptional.
Although we make immigration seem easy, we know it’s not enough for you to simply arrive . That’s why we partner with the world’s most accomplished mobility, documentation and relocation experts—the people who will help make your arrival as effortless and unstudied as your journey.
Many luxury brands that manage immigration in-house later find that their initial, home-grown approach no longer fits. As your brand expands, your immigration needs will too; we can step in at any stage in your brand’s growth and take over the immigration process straightaway, without creating extra work or effort for you or your employees.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
